feature_name: Harden db.json write concurrency + mobile logo top-left
description: ONE branch, TWO independent items kept as SEPARATE commits/tasks so the risky backend change and the cosmetic frontend change revert independently. ITEM 1 (high-risk, drives review rigor): structurally eliminate the db.json read-modify-write race CLASS that v1.8.0 patched finding-by-finding. Serialize all db.json persistence behind one mechanism (in-process async write-queue/mutex OR single in-memory source-of-truth with atomic serialized persistence — PE chooses and justifies, weighing write-queue/mutex vs. single in-memory SoT with debounced/atomic writes vs. whole-file-with-a-lock + fresh-read-inside-the-lock). Must: make concurrent RMW from ALL writer paths (runScanDirectories across ffmpeg awaits, POST /api/settings, POST /api/progress, recordServed, setTranscodeStatus, POST /api/config background scan) provably non-clobbering, proven by a regression test that interleaves settings + progress + recordServed + a scan and asserts nothing is lost (the v1.8.0 scan-clobber test at test/integration/scan-clobber.test.js is the template); preserve ALL v1.8.0 behavior + keep the 194-test suite green (incl. frozen suites like transcode-cache.test.js); ideally SIMPLIFY the runScanDirectories re-read-merge once a real lock exists but WITHOUT regressing the mount-loss guard / lastServedAt / transcodeStatus fixes (it is load-bearing); make on-disk saves atomic write-temp-then-rename — saveDatabase (server.js:73) is currently a plain fs.writeFileSync (crash mid-write can corrupt db.json). FOLD IN (same code area, both in tech-debt-tracker.md): (a) Active #3 — the FR3.4 dropped-rescan tail: when the coalesced rescan drain budget is exhausted with rescanRequested still set, schedule ONE deferred/rate-limited rescan via a short unref'd timer instead of dropping it; (b) Active #2 (OPTIONAL, only if PE finds it low-risk) — share ONE TRANSCODE_DIR enumeration between sweepAgedTranscodes + evictTranscodeCache instead of two back-to-back readdir+statSync walks. ITEM 2 (cosmetic, low-risk, SEPARATE commit): on MOBILE, move the logo TOP-LEFT on BOTH home and watch/video pages matching desktop; REMOVE the centered .mobile-logo app-shell logo (style.css mobile @media block ~1583-1631 sets header{flex-direction:column} + .header-left{width:100%;justify-content:center} + shows centered .mobile-logo). Desktop is already top-left — leave it. PM/PE decide what happens to mobile search once the logo is no longer centered above it (e.g. logo top-left with search beside/below it) — keep it clean and usable. Bottom-nav app-shell (Home/Playlists/Dark/Settings) STAYS. Preserve a11y (aria-labels, FileTube-home link), safe-area-inset-top, no theme/icon/appearance regression. [MANUAL]-visual (Dean confirms on-device). Repo: Node 22, node:test (npm test), ESLint 0 errors (11 'defined but never used' exported-globals warnings = allowed baseline), additive/zero-regression, ships to deantammam/filetube on a v*.*.* tag. Item 1 gets the FULL pipeline (discovery -> design -> implementation -> build) AND the two-reviewer QA gate (quality-assurance agent + separate /code-review) with v1.8.0 rigor.
npm_env_note: Every node/npm command in this repo needs `export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH"` first (fnm not auto-sourced). Applies to specialist agents running npm test / npm run lint.
prior_release: v1.8.0 settings-automation-cache shipped (stage done in prior state; see docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-05-settings-automation-cache.md). The 194-test suite is the green baseline. This feature makes the v1.8.0 race remediation (FR1 re-read-merge-on-save, FR3.1 unreadable-subtree, FR3.2 persistedServedAt, FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed, FR3.4 bounded rescan drain) STRUCTURAL rather than finding-by-finding.

tasks.title: Item 1 core: serialized updateDatabase(mutatorFn) + atomic saveDatabase + convert all 7 writers + collapse the scan re-read-merge
tasks.description: The headline hardening (two-reviewer QA gate). Add two building blocks to server.js and route all writes through them; readers UNCHANGED. (A) Atomic saveDatabase (server.js:73): rewrite to write a unique SAME-DIR temp file (`${DB_FILE}.${process.pid}.${seq++}.tmp`), fsync the fd, then renameSync over DB_FILE; on any failure clean up the temp (no orphan) and leave the original DB_FILE intact; signature unchanged (saveDatabase(db)); stays SYNCHRONOUS (the mutate-then-save must complete in one tick). (B) Serialized updateDatabase(mutatorFn): a module-level promise chain (let dbWriteChain=Promise.resolve()) where each call does `dbWriteChain.then(() => { const db=loadDatabase(); const result=mutatorFn(db); if(result!==false) saveDatabase(db); return result; })` and reassigns `dbWriteChain = run.catch(()=>{})` so a failed/throwing mutation is ISOLATED and never wedges the chain (caller's promise still rejects, next write proceeds). Contract: mutator is SYNCHRONOUS (no awaits), NON-REENTRANT (must not call updateDatabase), returns false to SKIP the save. Export updateDatabase for tests; place near saveDatabase. Convert all 7 writers per the design's exact recipes: (1) setTranscodeStatus (~415) — mutator sets status only when changed, else return false; (2) recordServed (~448) — KEEP the persistedServedAt ~10min throttle check synchronously BEFORE updateDatabase so throttled Range requests cost ZERO disk reads and never touch the lock; only the due branch enqueues a mutator that re-checks on-disk lastServedAt, sets it, updates the map (optional optimistic map set to coalesce a burst); (3) runScanDirectories final save — see the collapse below; (4) POST /api/config (~1038) — mutator sets db.folders/db.folderSettings from locally-computed validFolders/cleanSettings, respond with the locals (not db), then kick scanDirectories(); (5) POST /api/settings (~1116) — validation stays outside; capture prevInterval + merged `saved` settings via closure inside the mutator, then armScanTimer() only if scanIntervalMinutes changed, respond with settingsResponse(saved); (6) POST /api/progress (~1274) — 400 body validation stays outside, MOVE the 404 existence check INSIDE the mutator against the fresh db (set a notFound flag + return false, then 404 after await); (7) DELETE /api/videos/:id (~1296) — KEEP today's ordering for the 500-on-FS-failure contract: initial loadDatabase read for 404+filePath, FS unlinks inside the existing try/catch (throw => 500, db untouched), THEN updateDatabase deletes db.metadata[id]/db.progress[id]. recordServed + setTranscodeStatus stay fire-and-forget. COLLAPSE the scan Phase-2 re-read-merge: scan keeps its TWO-PHASE shape (NEVER hold the lock across FFmpeg awaits) — Phase 1 (no lock, unchanged): loadDatabase snapshot, awaited extraction building newMetadata, missingRoots/unreadablePaths, selectPrunableIds, idempotent FS cleanup of pruned thumbnails/transcodes; Phase 2 (ONE updateDatabase mutator): REMOVE the explicit `const fresh = loadDatabase()` (~872) — the mutator's db IS the fresh-inside-the-lock read; run the reconcile loop (root backfill + FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed from db.metadata[id] + reconcileTranscode) + mergeScannedMetadata(db.metadata,newMetadata) + db.progress prune + clearPersistedServedAt(id) inside it, return dbChanged. This provably CLOSES the hair-thin fresh-read->save window WITHOUT regressing: mount-loss guard (selectPrunableIds still off the scan's missingRoots), lastServedAt on-disk authority (mergeScannedMetadata only advances, never regresses), FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed (from the locked fresh db), bounded rescan drain (MAX_RESCAN_FOLLOWUPS unchanged — the tail itself is T2). reconcileTranscode is audited safe inside the mutator (only fs.existsSync reads + in-place mutation; no db writes, no queue kicks, non-reentrant). Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md's 'Re-read-merge-on-save' key-decision entry to the new contract: (i) all writers serialize through updateDatabase, (ii) saveDatabase is atomic write-temp-then-rename, (iii) the scan still merges into fresh state under the lock rather than overwriting a start-of-scan snapshot. Do NOT edit the completed v1.8.0 exec plan. Fold-in #2 (double readdir) is DEFERRED (not this task). The deferred-rescan tail (#3) is T2.
tasks.done_when: updateDatabase + atomic saveDatabase implemented per the design contract (serialized, non-reentrant, mutator-returns-false-skips-save, failed mutation isolated so the chain never wedges; temp-fsync-rename with on-fail cleanup + original intact). All 7 writers converted per the exact recipes (recordServed throttle BEFORE updateDatabase = zero disk read on throttled Range; /api/progress 404 inside mutator; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 ordering; /api/settings prevInterval/saved via closure + armScanTimer only on interval change). Scan re-read-merge collapsed into ONE Phase-2 mutator with the explicit fresh=loadDatabase removed and NO lock held across FFmpeg awaits. HEADLINE [INTEGRATION] test: settings + progress + recordServed + a scan interleaved in one tick, all awaited, ALL survive (settings change, progress entry, lastServedAt, and the scan's new-file metadata) — FAILS against pre-serialization code, PASSES after. [UNIT] atomic-write: forced write/rename failure leaves prior db.json intact + no *.tmp orphan; a successful save yields complete JSON + no leftover *.tmp. [UNIT] serialization-correctness: two back-to-back updateDatabase calls mutating DIFFERENT fields both survive, mutators run in enqueue order each seeing the prior's committed state. [INTEGRATION] v1.8.0 invariants re-verified: scan-clobber/scan-api/scan-prune/age-sweep green unmodified in behavior + targeted asserts that mount-loss guard, FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed, and lastServedAt authority still hold. transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN/byte-identical; all 194 existing tests green; every timer unref'd (no hung process; no lock held across an await so parallel node:test can't deadlock); ARCHITECTURE.md key-decision updated; lint 0 errors (no new warnings beyond the 11 baseline). npm env PATH exported before npm.
tasks.result: VERIFIED READY (build-specialist, deep scrutiny). lint 0; npm test 201/201; clean exit (no hang — mutex chain always settles, no lock held across an await). Confirmed: updateDatabase serialized primitive (fresh-read-INSIDE-lock, failure-isolated, chain never wedges); atomic saveDatabase (temp->fsync->rename; BOTH writeFileSync-fail and renameSync-fail paths leave db.json byte-identical + no orphan temp); all 7 writers converted correctly (recordServed throttle BEFORE lock = zero disk read on throttled Range; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 contract; POST /api/settings closure prevInterval + re-arm only on interval change; /api/progress 404 inside mutator); scan Phase-1 FFmpeg work OUTSIDE the lock, Phase-2 collapsed into ONE synchronous mutator with NO regression to mount-loss / lastServedAt / FR3.3 transcodeStatus / bounded drain; headline 4-way interleave test is a REAL guard (verified it fails against the bare-mutex stale-read flaw); transcode-cache.test.js byte-identical + readers unchanged. Diff scoped to server.js + docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + 4 test files.

tasks.title: Item 1 fold-in #3: deferred-rescan tail
tasks.description: Tech-debt Active #3. Add scheduleDeferredRescan(): a module-level single-guarded (`if (deferredRescanTimer) return;`), unref'd, ~5s (DEFERRED_RESCAN_DELAY_MS=5000) rate-limited setTimeout that clears its own handle then re-enters the already-bounded scanDirectories().catch(console.error). Hook it into scanDirectories's finally: read rescanRequested BEFORE clearing scanning, and call scheduleDeferredRescan() when it is still set (drain budget spent). Add a currentDeferredRescanTimer() test accessor. Bounded by construction: at most one pending timer, unref'd (never keeps the process/test alive), 5s rate-limited, re-enters the bounded scan — self-heals in discrete 5s passes under sustained demand, fires once and stops under bounded demand. Resolves the documented 'auto-scan Off + folder-add during the single follow-up pass' drop. Part of the HARDENING commit (may fold into T1's commit — EM/coordinator's call at commit time), NOT the logo commit. Fold-in #2 stays DEFERRED (tracker #2 open; its conditional AC is N/A).
tasks.done_when: [UNIT/INTEGRATION] with scanIntervalMinutes Off, drive scanDirectories so the drain exits with rescanRequested still true; exactly ONE unref'd timer scheduled (currentDeferredRescanTimer() non-null; a second exhaustion does NOT stack a second timer); running the deferred pass indexes the pending folder; timer cleared in teardown (suite exits clean, no hung process). The bounded rescan drain (MAX_RESCAN_FOLLOWUPS) is not regressed. transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN; full suite green (201 + new); lint 0. Also update tech-debt-tracker.md: move Active #3 to Closed with this resolution; leave Active #2 OPEN (deferred).
tasks.result: Implemented + build-verified and included in the two-reviewer QA gate on the combined T1+T2 hardening (suite 202, lint 0, transcode-cache.test.js frozen; scheduleDeferredRescan single-guarded/unref'd/5s, hooked in scanDirectories' finally; tech-debt #3 -> Closed, #2 left open). The gate verdict on the hardening was CHANGES REQUESTED — but the findings are error-handling REGRESSIONS in T1's route/persistence WIRING (both reviewers verified the core updateDatabase/atomic-write/scan-collapse ALGORITHM is correct by hand); T2's deferred-tail scope itself is clean. Fix round = HR1a + HR1b (see review_hr1).

tasks.title: Fix round 1 (mechanical): async-route try/catch + saveDatabase propagates + recordServed leak + loadDatabase backfill + startup temp sweep + comment softening
tasks.description: Cohesive server.js persistence/error-path fixes from the two-reviewer gate; the core updateDatabase/atomic-write mechanism is verified correct and must NOT be churned. (A) MUST-FIX regression: the 4 async write routes have NO try/catch — POST /api/config (server.js:1140), POST /api/settings (1250), POST /api/progress (1414), DELETE /api/videos/:id (1441) all became `async` + `await updateDatabase(...)` with no try/catch; Express 4 does not catch async rejections -> the request HANGS + an unhandledRejection fires (Node 22 can crash the process). Pre-hardening these were sync so Express caught the throw -> 500. Fix: wrap each `await updateDatabase(...)` (and for DELETE, keep the existing FS-unlink try/catch too) so a rejection returns a 500 JSON body, MIRRORING the existing POST /api/scan pattern (server.js:1184). ALSO add a top-level process.on('unhandledRejection', ...) [and consider 'uncaughtException'] logger as defense-in-depth. (B) MUST-FIX regression: saveDatabase (server.js:89-109) currently CATCHES openSync/writeFileSync/fsync/rename errors, logs, and returns normally -> updateDatabase RESOLVES success even when NOTHING persisted (route replies 200 {success:true} while db.json is unchanged; subsequent writers re-read the stale file = silent data loss). Fix: saveDatabase must PROPAGATE the failure (clean up the temp, then RETHROW) so updateDatabase REJECTS; combined with A's try/catch a disk failure becomes a clean 500 instead of false-success-or-hang. KEEP the temp cleanup + original-DB_FILE-intact behavior. (C) MUST-FIX regression: recordServed (server.js:524-548) now does persistedServedAt.set(id, now) at line 535 BEFORE the in-lock existence check, so a serve/transcode-completion recordServed(id) for an id concurrently DELETEd/pruned inserts a throttle-map entry no cleanup reclaims -> unbounded growth under delete-while-streaming churn + re-add suppression (re-opens the FR3.2 leak; reachable via the transcode close-callback recordServed with no same-tick existence guard). Fix: only set persistedServedAt when the entry actually exists / only when actually persisting — move the .set inside/after the mutator confirms the entry (or guard it), restoring the old ordering. ROBUSTNESS (closes A/B's trigger at the source): loadDatabase (server.js:48-71) backfills only folderSettings + settings, NOT metadata/progress/folders — a valid-JSON legacy/hand-edited db.json missing `metadata` or `progress` makes the mutators throw TypeError (the concrete trigger for A). Fix loadDatabase to backfill ALL top-level keys (folders:[], folderSettings:{}, progress:{}, metadata:{}, settings:defaults) on the read path so a partial db.json can't throw; also consider guarding the initial-file fs.writeFileSync at server.js:57 (outside the try/catch — an EACCES/ENOSPC there throws; A+B otherwise cover it). (E) SHOULD-FIX (low): a SIGKILL/OOM between openSync(tmp) and rename leaves an orphan db.json.<pid>.<seq>.tmp; there is no startup sweep (unlike cleanupOrphanTmp for *.tmp.mp4 at server.js:205) and the saveDatabase comment overclaims 'no orphan left behind'. Fix: add a startup sweep of DATA_DIR for db.json.*.tmp (mirror cleanupOrphanTmp) + correct the comment. (F) SHOULD-FIX (low): saveDatabase fsyncs the temp fd but not the DATA_DIR directory after rename, so a POWER-loss right after rename can revert (not corrupt) to the old db.json; threat model per RELIABILITY.md is process-crash not power-loss, so SOFTEN the durability comment to not overclaim power-loss durability (a directory fsync is optional, not required). Tests: A -> each of the 4 routes returns 500 JSON (not hang/crash) when its updateDatabase rejects (e.g. stub saveDatabase to throw); B -> saveDatabase rethrows on write/rename failure and db.json prior content stays intact + no orphan temp; C -> recordServed for a NON-EXISTENT id leaves NO persistedServedAt entry; loadDatabase -> a partial db.json (missing metadata/progress) loads with all keys backfilled and mutators don't throw. D (membership resurrection) is HR1b, NOT this task.
tasks.done_when: All 4 async write routes (config/settings/progress/videos:id) wrap their await updateDatabase in try/catch -> 500 JSON on rejection (mirroring POST /api/scan); a top-level unhandledRejection [+ optionally uncaughtException] logger is added. saveDatabase PROPAGATES/rethrows on failure (temp cleaned, original intact) so updateDatabase rejects; a disk failure yields a clean 500 not false-success. recordServed no longer inserts a persistedServedAt entry for a non-existent/deleted id (leak closed; FR3.2 not re-opened). loadDatabase backfills ALL top-level keys so a partial valid-JSON db.json can't throw in a mutator. Startup sweep removes orphan db.json.*.tmp; saveDatabase comment corrected (no orphan overclaim) and the durability comment softened (no power-loss overclaim). New tests cover A/B/C + the loadDatabase backfill. The core updateDatabase primitive + atomic temp->fsync->rename mechanism is UNCHANGED in behavior (not churned). transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN; full suite green (202 + new); every timer unref'd; lint 0; PATH export before npm.
tasks.result: VERIFIED READY (build-specialist). lint 0; npm test 212/212; clean exit twice (process-level handlers don't keep the runner alive). All fixes confirmed REAL guards: A (4 routes try/catch -> 500 with disk-failure-injection tests; process.on unhandledRejection/uncaughtException log-only), B (saveDatabase rethrows -> updateDatabase rejects on disk failure, original intact, no orphan), C (recordServed sets persistedServedAt only when the entry exists — no-leak test fails against old ordering, hot-path throttle intact), loadDatabase backfills ALL top-level keys (partial-db test passes), E (cleanupOrphanDbTmp startup sweep), F (durability comment softened). CONFIRMED UNTOUCHED: updateDatabase primitive body, scan Phase-2 membership reconcile / mergeScannedMetadata, transcode-cache.test.js (empty diff). All v1.8.0 + hardening headline tests still green.

tasks.title: Fix round 1 (subtle, PE note first): D — DELETE-vs-scan membership resurrection
tasks.description: SHOULD-FIX, PRE-EXISTING in v1.8.0 but DECISION = FIX IT (not tech-debt-track) because it is the SAME stale-snapshot clobber class this branch is named for closing, just at the MEMBERSHIP dimension — leaving it would undercut the 'class eliminated' headline claim. EM flags this decision to Dean via the coordinator. The scan Phase-2 mutator (server.js:1003-1031) sets fresh.metadata = mergeScannedMetadata(fresh.metadata, newMetadata) where membership is taken WHOLESALE from newMetadata, built in Phase-1 (outside the lock, server.js ~985+) from a pre-delete snapshot. A DELETE /api/videos/:id that commits DURING the scan is UNDONE — the id is resurrected as a dangling entry (file gone, entry back) until the next prune scan. Because this edits the subtle, load-bearing Phase-2 reconcile that interacts with mergeScannedMetadata + the mount-loss guard + the prune set, it gets a SHORT PE design note FIRST (like v1.8.0 FR3.3), then an SDE task. Proposed rule for the PE to nail: in the Phase-2 mutator, reconcile membership against the FRESH db — an id in newMetadata that WAS present in the Phase-1 db snapshot but is NOW ABSENT from fresh.metadata was deleted concurrently -> do NOT resurrect (drop it); a genuinely-new id the scan found (absent from BOTH the Phase-1 snapshot AND fresh) is still added. This needs capturing the Phase-1 snapshot's metadata id-set. PE confirms the rule doesn't regress mount-loss retention / lastServedAt authority / FR3.3 seed / the prune path, and specifies the exact change + test. SDE task follows after HR1a build-verifies (HR1a edits routes/saveDatabase/recordServed/loadDatabase/startup; D edits the scan mutator — no code collision, but sequence for a clean intermediate + the note must land first).
tasks.done_when: PE note appended to the exec plan's Design section specifying the membership-reconciliation rule + confirming no regression to mount-loss/lastServedAt/FR3.3/prune. SDE implements it in the Phase-2 mutator (drop a concurrently-deleted id rather than resurrect; still add genuinely-new scanned ids). Regression test: a DELETE /api/videos/:id committing DURING a scan leaves the entry DELETED (not resurrected) after the scan save — FAILS pre-fix, PASSES after. mergeScannedMetadata behavior for the non-concurrent case unchanged. transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN; full suite green; lint 0; PATH export before npm.
tasks.pe_rule: PE note complete (in the exec plan). Reconciliation is a single per-id test keyed on 'was this id in the Phase-1 snapshot?': (in-snapshot AND now absent from fresh.metadata) = concurrently DELETEd -> DROP from newMetadata; (still in fresh, incl. mount-loss-retained) = KEEP; (absent from BOTH snapshot and fresh) = genuinely-new -> ADD. Impl: capture `const phase1Ids = new Set(oldIds)` at Phase-1 (server.js:995, db is the scan-start snapshot), close it into the Phase-2 mutator; add a drop loop over Object.keys(newMetadata) that deletes `newMetadata[id]` when `phase1Ids.has(id) && !hasOwnProperty(fresh.metadata, id)`, placed immediately BEFORE `fresh.metadata = mergeScannedMetadata(fresh.metadata, newMetadata)` (~1021). mergeScannedMetadata stays byte-unchanged. Preserves mount-loss guard (a retained entry is still in fresh -> never dropped), lastServedAt authority, FR3.3 seed.
tasks.result: VERIFIED READY (build-specialist) + committed in HR1 commit 7520d60. Finding-D drop loop implemented per the PE rule (phase1Ids capture + drop-before-merge; mergeScannedMetadata byte-unchanged). Headline DELETE-during-scan test confirmed a real guard (id stays deleted; fails pre-fix); mount-loss-retained not wrongly dropped; genuinely-new file still added; non-concurrent case unchanged. transcode-cache.test.js frozen. The subsequent FOCUSED re-review of the HR1 commit CONVERGED (3 findings -> 2): one hot-path regression finding-C's fix introduced (-> HR2), one minor self-healing same-path re-add edge (-> tech-debt #4). Coordinator DECISION: fix HR2, tech-debt-track #4, then STOP the review cycle (two-reviewer gate + 2 re-review passes, each finding strictly smaller things — diminishing returns).

tasks.title: Fix round 2 (small): restore the recordServed up-front throttle without re-leaking (finding-C fix regression)
tasks.description: HOT-PATH regression that finding-C's fix (HR1a) introduced (CONFIRMED, server.js 582-601). The finding-C leak fix moved persistedServedAt.set(id, now) from BEFORE the updateDatabase enqueue to INSIDE the mutator (now at 594/598). Consequence: while dbWriteChain is backlogged (e.g. during a scan), persistedServedAt.get(id) stays undefined, so a burst of same-id /video Range requests each pass the hot-path short-circuit (585) and enqueue their OWN updateDatabase -> each does a full synchronous loadDatabase() in the lock. This reintroduces the redundant-read throttle regression the v1.8.0 persistedServedAt map was built to prevent. Bounded (read-amplification, drains turn-by-turn; only the first commit writes) but on the streaming hot path and reachable whenever a scan/backlog overlaps streaming. THE FIX must satisfy BOTH the throttle AND finding-C's no-leak: (1) restore the up-front OPTIMISTIC persistedServedAt.set(id, now) BEFORE the enqueue (after the hot-path short-circuit at 585) so a burst de-dups (2..N short-circuit); (2) in the mutator, when db.metadata[id] does NOT exist (the `if (!entry) return false` branch at 588), persistedServedAt.delete(id) to UNDO the optimistic set so a concurrently-deleted id leaves no leaked entry (preserves finding-C). Keep the hot-path short-circuit (585) and the already-fresh mutator branch (593-596). DO NOT touch updateDatabase/mergeScannedMetadata bodies, HR1a's route try/catch, or HR1b's drop loop.
tasks.done_when: recordServed sets persistedServedAt optimistically BEFORE the enqueue (restoring burst de-dup) AND deletes it in the mutator's no-entry branch (no re-leak). Tests: (a) the existing finding-C no-leak test (recordServed for a non-existent id -> NO persistedServedAt entry after the call) STILL passes with the undo; (b) NEW test — a burst of N same-id recordServed calls while a write is queued enqueues only ONE updateDatabase (2..N short-circuit on the hot path), verified to FAIL against the current in-mutator-only ordering. Hot-path short-circuit + already-fresh branch intact. updateDatabase/mergeScannedMetadata bodies + HR1a route try/catch + HR1b drop loop UNTOUCHED. transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN; full suite green (216 + new); lint 0; PATH export before npm.

tasks.title: Item 2 (SEPARATE commit): mobile logo top-left
tasks.description: CSS-only, low-risk, its OWN commit (not the hardening commit); no two-reviewer QA gate, build-specialist verify + Dean [MANUAL] on-device only. In public/css/style.css's mobile @media (max-width:768px) 'Mobile app shell: header restructure' block (~1583-1631), two edits: (1) .header-left: justify-content center -> flex-start (keep width:100%, gap:0); (2) .mobile-logo img: margin 0 auto -> 0 (keep display:block, height/width:28px). Header stays flex-direction:column so .header-search stays width:100% full-width on the row BELOW the logo. Update the block's lead comment from 'Centered logo...' to 'Top-left logo + full-width search below it'. .logo stays hidden, .mobile-logo stays shown, .header-right stays hidden, bottom-nav untouched. Covers home (index.html) + watch (watch.html) via the shared header/stylesheet; also check setup.html (same header, no .header-search) for visual consistency. Preserve a11y (aria-label='FileTube home', tab order — DOM untouched), safe-area-inset-top (header padding/min-height untouched), no theme/icon-set regression. Desktop (>768px) provably unchanged (edit inside the mobile media query). No public/js/*.js selector breaks (no class/id/DOM change).
tasks.done_when: [MANUAL] logo renders top-left on home + watch (matching desktop), search usable full-width below, aria-label/link-to-home + tab order unchanged, safe-area-inset-top respected, no icon-set/theme regression, desktop unchanged — Dean confirms on-device. [PROCESS] lint 0 (0 new warnings) + npm test green (no existing test asserting header markup breaks). Change scoped to the mobile media query only.

grounding.saveDatabase: server.js:73 — plain fs.writeFileSync(DB_FILE, JSON.stringify(db,null,2)); NOT atomic. 7 saveDatabase() call sites confirmed: lines 419, 460, 899, 1041, 1144, 1291, 1329.
grounding.scan_clobber_template: test/integration/scan-clobber.test.js (v1.8.0 FR1 headline). Also merge/lifecycle tests in test/integration/scan-api.test.js, test/integration/scan-prune.test.js, test/integration/age-sweep.test.js.
grounding.mobile_logo_css: public/css/style.css:1583-1631 (mobile @media block: header flex-direction:column, .header-left width:100% justify-content:center, .logo display:none, .mobile-logo display:block centered via margin:0 auto, .header-right display:none). Desktop header (top-left) unchanged.
grounding.tech_debt_fold_in: docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md Active #3 (FR3.4 dropped-rescan tail -> deferred unref'd rescan) FOLD IN; Active #2 (double readdir+statSync over TRANSCODE_DIR) OPTIONAL fold-in per PE risk call.

review_hr1.verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED (two-reviewer gate on the combined T1+T2 hardening). BOTH reviewers verified the CORE primitive (updateDatabase mutex, atomic saveDatabase, the 7 conversions' contracts, the scan-collapse invariants) is CORRECT by hand — the algorithm is sound. They converged on a cluster of ERROR-HANDLING regressions in the route/persistence WIRING that a green suite missed. Keep the core mechanism intact (don't churn it). Split: HR1a = mechanical cluster (A/B/C + loadDatabase backfill + E/F); HR1b = D (subtle, PE note first).
review_hr1.A_async_routes_no_trycatch: MUST-FIX regression (both reviewers, 4 findings). POST /api/config (1140), POST /api/settings (1250), POST /api/progress (1414), DELETE /api/videos/:id (1441) became async + await updateDatabase with NO try/catch; Express 4 doesn't catch async rejections -> request HANGS + unhandledRejection (Node 22 can crash). Pre-hardening they were sync -> Express caught the throw -> 500. Fix: try/catch -> 500 JSON mirroring POST /api/scan (1184) + a top-level unhandledRejection [+ uncaughtException] logger.
review_hr1.B_saveDatabase_swallows_errors: MUST-FIX regression (server.js 89-109). Catches disk-write errors, logs, returns normally -> updateDatabase RESOLVES success though NOTHING persisted -> 200 {success:true} while db.json unchanged + subsequent writers re-read stale (silent data loss). Fix: PROPAGATE (rethrow after temp cleanup) so updateDatabase rejects; with A -> clean 500. Keep temp cleanup + original intact.
review_hr1.C_recordServed_leak: MUST-FIX/PLAUSIBLE (server.js 535, findings 5+10). persistedServedAt.set(id,now) now BEFORE the in-lock existence check -> a serve/transcode-completion for a concurrently DELETEd/pruned id inserts a throttle-map entry no cleanup reclaims (unbounded growth + re-add suppression, re-opens FR3.2). Reachable via the transcode close-callback recordServed with no same-tick guard. Fix: only set when the entry exists / only when persisting.
review_hr1.loadDatabase_backfill: ROBUSTNESS (closes A/B trigger at source). loadDatabase backfills only folderSettings+settings, NOT metadata/progress/folders -> a partial valid-JSON db.json makes mutators throw TypeError (the concrete A trigger). Fix: backfill ALL top-level keys on read. Also the initial-file fs.writeFileSync at 57 is OUTSIDE the try/catch (EACCES/ENOSPC throws) — A+B cover it, guard optional.
review_hr1.D_delete_vs_scan_resurrection: SHOULD-FIX, PRE-EXISTING (server.js 1021, finding 2). Phase-2 mutator takes membership WHOLESALE from newMetadata (built Phase-1 pre-delete, outside the lock) -> a DELETE committing DURING the scan is undone (id resurrected as a dangling entry until the next prune). SAME clobber class the branch closes, at the membership dimension. EM DECISION: FIX IT (supports the 'class eliminated' claim) via a SHORT PE note first (subtle Phase-2 reconcile) -> HR1b. Rule: drop a newMetadata id that was in the Phase-1 snapshot but is now ABSENT from fresh (concurrently deleted); still add genuinely-new scanned ids.
review_hr1.E_orphan_db_tmp: SHOULD-FIX (low, server.js 90). SIGKILL/OOM between openSync(tmp) and rename leaves an orphan db.json.<pid>.<seq>.tmp; no startup sweep (unlike cleanupOrphanTmp 205); comment overclaims 'no orphan'. Fix: startup sweep of db.json.*.tmp + correct comment. (HR1a)
review_hr1.F_durability_comment_overclaim: SHOULD-FIX (low, server.js 100). fsyncs the temp fd but not DATA_DIR after rename -> power-loss right after rename can REVERT (not corrupt) to old db.json. Threat model = process-crash not power-loss (RELIABILITY.md) -> SOFTEN the comment; directory fsync optional. (HR1a)
review_hr1.re_review_plan: After HR1a build-verifies (then HR1b's PE note + SDE), coordinator runs a FOCUSED re-review of the HR1 hunks; if clean -> T3 (logo) + PR.

history.note: New feature initiated after v1.8.0 (settings-automation-cache) shipped/done. Prior state archived by overwrite; v1.8.0 exec plan is in docs/exec-plans/completed/. Read CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, RELIABILITY.md, tech-debt-tracker.md, the mobile CSS block, and grounded the two items in real code before routing: (1) saveDatabase (server.js:73) is a plain non-atomic writeFileSync with 7 call sites — the crash-safety atomic-write ask is a real gap; the v1.8.0 re-read-merge (FR1) still has a hair-thin lost-update window and is finding-by-finding, so a single serialized persistence path is the structural fix. (2) Mobile centered-logo confirmed at style.css:1583-1631. Tech-debt Active #2 (double readdir) and #3 (dropped-rescan tail) confirmed in the tracker for fold-in. ONE branch harden/db-writes-and-logo, TWO independent commits (Item 1 high-risk hardening drives the two-reviewer gate; Item 2 cosmetic mobile logo). Target v1.9.0.

Coordinator explicitly authorized starting Discovery and asked the EM to return with requirements/acceptance criteria + open questions (esp. the PE's proposed hardening approach). Advanced bootstrap -> discovery. Wrote product-manager inbox at .state/inbox/product-manager.md grounding the PM in: the two-item split with SEPARATE-commit constraint; the full writer-path list + the non-clobbering interleave regression test requirement; the atomic-write gap at saveDatabase:73; the fold-in of tech-debt #2/#3; the v1.8.0 behavior/tests that MUST be preserved; and the mobile logo decision (already made by Dean — do NOT re-ask; decide only the search layout). Asked the PM to keep D1-mobile-logo settled, tag every AC [UNIT]/[INTEGRATION]/[MANUAL]/[PROCESS], keep the two items' AC in separate sections, and surface the PE hardening-approach choice as the headline open question for the design gate (write-queue/mutex vs. in-memory SoT vs. locked whole-file). Exec plan to be written to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-05-harden-db-writes-and-logo.md; artifacts stay null until the PM produces it. Awaiting PM completion, then /prep-pe-design.

Design complete: PE wrote the ## Design section into docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-05-harden-db-writes-and-logo.md and set artifacts.design to that path. HEADLINE RECOMMENDATION (for the design-gate sign-off): a serialized in-process updateDatabase(mutatorFn) primitive — an async-mutex (promise chain) that per write loads a FRESH db inside the lock, applies the synchronous mutator, and atomically saves; all 7 writers route through it. This is approach (c) [locked whole-file + fresh-read-inside-lock] packaged as approach (a)'s clean primitive; it provably kills the RMW clobber class (read+write in one critical section) at the LOWEST blast radius. Rejected (b) single in-memory SoT (rewrites all ~15 loadDatabase() reader contracts + crash-divergence risk) and bare (a) (leaves stale-read window unless the read moves inside the lock — which IS the chosen primitive). Decisive win vs (b): readers are UNCHANGED (loadDatabase stays; atomic rename means no torn reads). ALL saves become atomic write-temp-then-rename (fsync + same-dir temp + rename + on-fail cleanup). The v1.8.0 re-read-merge SIMPLIFIES (subsumed): scan keeps its two-phase shape (never holds the lock across FFmpeg awaits); the explicit fresh=loadDatabase() is removed and the reconcile+mergeScannedMetadata+progress-prune+save collapse into ONE updateDatabase mutator, provably closing the hair-thin fresh-read->save window — mount-loss guard, lastServedAt authority, FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed, and bounded rescan drain all preserved with tests kept. recordServed's persistedServedAt throttle stays a throttle-only check BEFORE updateDatabase (no per-Range disk read/lock). Fold-in #3 (MANDATORY) designed: deferred-rescan tail via a single-guarded, unref'd, 5s rate-limited setTimeout re-entering the bounded scanDirectories. Fold-in #2 (double readdir) DEFERRED — would touch the frozen transcode-cache path; tracker #2 stays open, conditional AC N/A. Tests specified: headline 4-way interleave (fail-pre/pass-post), atomic-write unit, serialization-correctness, v1.8.0-invariant re-verify, deferred-rescan-tail. Item 2 (mobile logo): two-line CSS change in the mobile @media block (.header-left justify-content center->flex-start; .mobile-logo img margin 0 auto->0), search stays full-width below, desktop/a11y/safe-area/bottom-nav untouched. Recommended task split: Task 1 core serialize+atomic+7-writer conversion+scan simplify (two-reviewer QA gate); Task 2 fold-in #3 deferred tail; Task 3 Item 2 logo — Item 1 and Item 2 as SEPARATE commits. Noted ARCHITECTURE.md's re-read-merge-on-save key-decision must be updated WITH Task 1 to describe the new contract. No new runtime dependency (hand-rolled promise chain suffices). Next: EM relays the recommended approach to Dean for the design-gate sign-off, then /prep-em-tasks.

Discovery complete: PM wrote docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-05-harden-db-writes-and-logo.md (both items separate sections; Item 1 = 8 tagged AC incl. the headline non-clobbering interleave test modeled on scan-clobber.test.js, atomic write-temp-then-rename behavior, explicit v1.8.0-invariant re-verification, mandatory deferred-rescan-tail fold-in #3, and conditional shared-enumeration #2; Item 2 = 7 [MANUAL] + 1 [PROCESS] AC, search-full-width-below-logo proposed default). artifacts.requirements/exec_plan already set by PM to that path. No new product decision surfaced; logo decision stays locked; search sub-question defaulted. Advanced discovery -> design per coordinator. Wrote principal-engineer inbox at .state/inbox/principal-engineer.md. HEADLINE design deliverable = the PE's RECOMMENDED serialization approach + justification, to be relayed to Dean at the design gate BEFORE build (it reshapes how the whole app reads/writes db.json). Framed the three approaches with the coordinator's sharpened tradeoffs: (a) write-queue/mutex — but the READ must also move inside the lock at every site or the stale-read window remains (lower-blast-radius is partly illusory); (b) single in-memory SoT — most fully eliminates the race but changes every loadDatabase() reader's contract; (c) locked whole-file + fresh-read-inside-the-lock — closest evolution of the v1.8.0 re-read-merge but keeps full-file I/O per write and little complexity reduction. ALL three must also deliver atomic write-temp-then-rename for saveDatabase. Asked the PE to specify: chosen approach + why; how each of the 7 writer sites + all readers change; how the v1.8.0 re-read-merge simplifies-or-stays WITHOUT regressing mount-loss/lastServedAt/transcodeStatus; the deferred-rescan-tail (#3) impl; the double-readdir (#2) fold-or-defer call; the atomic-write impl; coherence with recordServed's in-memory persistedServedAt throttle; and the regression tests. Plus an Item 2 design (logo top-left, search full-width below) and a task breakdown keeping hardening and logo as SEPARATE tasks/commits. Emphasized: high-risk persistence-layer code — conservative design, all 194 tests green, frozen suites untouched. artifacts.design stays null until the PE writes the ## Design section. Coordinator will run the principal-engineer and relay the recommended approach to Dean for a design-gate sign-off before implementation; then /prep-em-tasks.

DESIGN GATE PASSED: Dean signed off — GO with the PE's serialized updateDatabase(mutatorFn) approach (locked load-fresh-inside-lock -> synchronous mutate -> atomic write-temp-then-rename; all 7 writers routed through it; readers unchanged; scan re-read-merge collapses into the mutator with FFmpeg work OUTSIDE the lock; fold in #3 deferred-rescan tail; #2 double-readdir deferred). Approved as-is, no changes. EM-owned task-breakdown stage done: adopted the PE's recommended split, recorded T1/T2/T3 in state.tasks (each with description/files/done_when/status) and wrote the ## Task breakdown section into the exec plan. Item 1 (hardening) and Item 2 (logo) stay SEPARATE commits: T1 = the core serialize+atomic+7-writer-conversion+scan-collapse (two-reviewer QA gate), T2 = fold-in #3 deferred-rescan tail (same hardening commit as T1 or a follow-on — coordinator's call at commit time, NOT the logo commit), T3 = Item 2 mobile logo (its own commit, build-verify + Dean [MANUAL] only). Advanced design -> implementation; set T1 status=in-progress (T2/T3 pending). Wrote software-developer inbox at .state/inbox/software-developer.md for T1 ONLY, grounded in the design's exact updateDatabase/saveDatabase snippets + the 7 per-site conversion recipes + the two-phase scan collapse (remove explicit fresh=loadDatabase at ~872; lock wraps only the synchronous merge+save, never the FFmpeg awaits) + the required ARCHITECTURE.md key-decision update + all 4 test groups (headline 4-way interleave fail-pre/pass-post, atomic-write unit, serialization-correctness, v1.8.0-invariant re-verify). Hard constraints reiterated: mutator synchronous + non-reentrant + returns-false-skips-save + failed-mutation-isolated; recordServed persistedServedAt throttle check BEFORE updateDatabase (zero disk read on throttled Range); /api/progress 404 inside the mutator; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 ordering; /api/settings prevInterval/saved via closure + armScanTimer only on interval change; no lock held across an await (parallel node:test can't deadlock); all timers unref'd; transcode-cache.test.js FROZEN; all 194 tests green; lint 0; PATH export before npm. Scoped OUT of T1: the deferred-rescan tail (=T2), the logo CSS (=T3), fold-in #2 (deferred). Coordinator will run the software-developer against T1, then drive the per-task implement->build loop + the two-reviewer gate on the hardening. Awaiting T1 completion, then /prep-build-verify.

T1 (core hardening) DONE + build-verified with DEEP SCRUTINY (coordinator-relayed): build-specialist verdict VERIFIED READY. lint 0; npm test 201/201; clean exit (no hang — mutex chain always settles, no lock held across an await). Confirmed: updateDatabase serialized primitive (fresh-read-INSIDE-lock, failure-isolated, chain never wedges); atomic saveDatabase (temp->fsync->rename; BOTH writeFileSync-fail and renameSync-fail paths leave db.json byte-identical + no orphan temp); all 7 writers converted correctly (recordServed throttle BEFORE lock = zero disk read on throttled Range; DELETE keeps FS-unlink-before-db 500 contract; POST /api/settings closure prevInterval + re-arm only on interval change; /api/progress 404 inside mutator); scan Phase-1 FFmpeg work OUTSIDE the lock, Phase-2 collapsed into ONE synchronous mutator with NO regression to mount-loss / lastServedAt / FR3.3 transcodeStatus / bounded drain; headline 4-way interleave test is a REAL guard (fails against the bare-mutex stale-read flaw); transcode-cache.test.js byte-identical + readers unchanged. Diff scoped to server.js + docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + 4 test files. Marked T1 status=done with result recorded. Advanced to T2 (deferred-rescan tail, tech-debt #3, part of the HARDENING commit — NOT the logo): set T2 status=in-progress. Re-read the CURRENT drain seam (line-shifted by T1): scanState (server.js:777), MAX_RESCAN_FOLLOWUPS (789), scanDirectories (804) with the do/while drain (812-816) and the finally clearing scanning (817-820) — the bounded drain exits with scanState.rescanRequested possibly STILL true (budget spent), which is exactly the FR3.4 drop the tail fixes. Wrote software-developer inbox for T2 ONLY: add scheduleDeferredRescan() — module-level single-guarded (`if (deferredRescanTimer) return;`), unref'd, DEFERRED_RESCAN_DELAY_MS=5000 setTimeout that nulls its own handle then re-enters scanDirectories().catch(console.error); hook it into scanDirectories's finally by reading scanState.rescanRequested BEFORE clearing scanning and calling scheduleDeferredRescan() when still set; add currentDeferredRescanTimer() accessor. Bounded/no-livelock (drain still bounded per invocation; at most one pending timer; 5s rate-limited; re-enters the bounded scan) and no-dangling-timer/clean-exit under node:test (unref + teardown clear). Regression test: drain budget exhausted + rescan still pending => exactly one deferred follow-up fires and indexes the pending work, no dangling timer/clean exit, no second stacked timer on a second exhaustion. Also move tech-debt Active #3 -> Closed; leave Active #2 (double readdir) OPEN/DEFERRED per the design. Constraints reiterated: full suite green (201 + new), transcode-cache.test.js frozen, PATH export before npm, do NOT touch T3 (logo). Awaiting T2 completion, then /prep-build-verify; after T2 build-verifies, the two-reviewer QA gate runs on the COMBINED T1+T2 hardening, then T3 (logo).

T2 (deferred-rescan tail) implemented + build-verified (suite 202, lint 0, tech-debt #3 -> Closed, #2 left open) — marked T2 status=done. The TWO-REVIEWER QA GATE then ran on the COMBINED T1+T2 hardening: verdict CHANGES REQUESTED. BOTH reviewers verified the CORE primitive (updateDatabase mutex, atomic saveDatabase, the 7 conversions' contracts, the scan-collapse invariants) is CORRECT by hand — the algorithm is sound — but converged on a cluster of ERROR-HANDLING regressions in the route/persistence WIRING that a green suite missed. Full triage in state.review_hr1. Verified each against CURRENT server.js: (A) the 4 async write routes have NO try/catch (config 1140, settings 1250, progress 1414, videos:id 1441) -> Express 4 doesn't catch async rejections -> hang + unhandledRejection (regression: pre-hardening sync -> Express caught -> 500); (B) saveDatabase (89-109) swallows disk errors -> updateDatabase resolves false-success while db.json unchanged (silent data loss); (C) recordServed (535) sets persistedServedAt BEFORE the in-lock existence check -> leak for a concurrently-deleted id (re-opens FR3.2); plus loadDatabase (48-71) backfills only folderSettings+settings not metadata/progress/folders (a partial db.json throws in a mutator — A's concrete trigger); (D) PRE-EXISTING DELETE-vs-scan membership resurrection in the Phase-2 mutator (1021, mergeScannedMetadata takes membership wholesale from the pre-delete newMetadata); (E) orphan db.json.*.tmp on hard crash (no startup sweep); (F) durability comment overclaims power-loss. Set stage back to implementation (fix round HR1). TASK BREAKDOWN (EM-owned): HR1a = the MECHANICAL cohesive cluster A+B+C+loadDatabase-backfill+E+F as ONE SDE task (all server.js persistence/error paths; core mechanism NOT churned) with tests for A/B/C + the backfill; HR1b = D (subtle Phase-2 membership reconcile). EM DECISION on D: FIX IT (not tech-debt-track) because it is the same stale-snapshot clobber class the branch is named for closing, at the membership dimension — leaving it undercuts the 'class eliminated' claim; flagged to Dean via the coordinator. Because D edits the subtle load-bearing Phase-2 reconcile, it gets a SHORT PE note FIRST (like v1.8.0 FR3.3), then an SDE task AFTER HR1a build-verifies (no code collision — HR1a is routes/saveDatabase/recordServed/loadDatabase/startup, D is the scan mutator — but sequence for a clean intermediate + the note must land first). Recorded HR1a (in-progress) + HR1b (pending) in state.tasks. Wrote TWO inboxes this turn since they're independent: (1) software-developer inbox at .state/inbox/software-developer.md = HR1a mechanical cluster, grounded in the exact seams (4 route line numbers + the POST /api/scan try/catch template at 1184; saveDatabase 89-109 rethrow; recordServed 524-548 ordering; loadDatabase 48-71 backfill-all-keys + the initial-writeFileSync at 57; cleanupOrphanTmp 205 template for the db.json.*.tmp sweep); (2) principal-engineer inbox at .state/inbox/principal-engineer.md = the SHORT single-issue D note (design-only, no code, no conflict with HR1a) specifying the membership-reconciliation rule (drop a newMetadata id present in the Phase-1 snapshot but now ABSENT from fresh = concurrently deleted; still add genuinely-new scanned ids; needs the Phase-1 metadata id-set) + confirm no regression to mount-loss/lastServedAt/FR3.3/prune + the DELETE-during-scan test. SEQUENCING for the coordinator: run the HR1a SDE now AND the D PE note in parallel (independent); HR1b's SDE comes AFTER HR1a build-verifies AND the PE note lands. Constraints reiterated to both: core updateDatabase/atomic-write mechanism intact (verified — don't churn), transcode-cache.test.js frozen, full suite green (202 + new), every timer unref'd, PATH export before npm, don't touch T3 (logo). Awaiting HR1a completion + the D PE note, then /prep-build-verify for HR1a; after HR1a+HR1b build-verify -> focused re-review of the HR1 hunks -> T3 (logo) -> PR.

HR1b (finding D) PE DESIGN NOTE landed: appended '### HR1b (finding D) — DELETE-vs-scan membership reconciliation' to the exec plan's ## Design section (design-only, no code; no collision with HR1a). CONFIRMED the proposed rule and specified it precisely as a single per-id test in a decision table: an id in the Phase-1 snapshot but now ABSENT from the fresh in-lock db => concurrently DELETEd => DROP from newMetadata (don't resurrect); an id present in fresh (incl. mount-loss/unreadable/toggle-off RETAINED entries) => KEEP; an id NOT in the Phase-1 snapshot (genuinely-new scanned file) => KEEP/add. The 'was in Phase-1 snapshot' column is the load-bearing discriminator that separates a genuine concurrent delete (drop) from a genuinely-new file (add) and a mount-loss-retained entry (keep) — the naive 'absent-from-fresh => drop' rule is WRONG (eats new files). CAPTURE: `const phase1Ids = new Set(oldIds)` right at the existing `oldIds = Object.keys(db.metadata)` (server.js ~942, the scan-start snapshot), closed over into the Phase-2 mutator — no new read/lock. EXACT CHANGE: a one-loop drop step in the Phase-2 mutator immediately BEFORE `fresh.metadata = mergeScannedMetadata(fresh.metadata, newMetadata)` (~1021), deleting from newMetadata any id where `phase1Ids.has(id) && !hasOwnProperty(fresh.metadata, id)`; mergeScannedMetadata stays BYTE-UNCHANGED (lastServedAt-max contract pure/auditable). NON-REGRESSION argued: mount-loss guard (retained entry is present in fresh => row 2 KEEP; drop keys off 'absent from fresh'=genuine delete, never 'unscanned this pass'; selectPrunableIds untouched), lastServedAt authority (merge still only advances; dropped ids already absent from fresh), FR3.3 transcodeStatus seed (unchanged), prune path (orthogonal — a concurrently-deleted id was surviving at snapshot time so NOT in prunable; drop closes exactly prune's blind spot; DELETE already removed fresh.progress[id]). RESIDUAL EDGES noted: same-path re-add mid-scan (same md5 id — stale Z dropped this scan, new file at P indexed next scan, correct); reconcileTranscode probing a to-be-dropped id is harmless (existsSync-only, mutation never reaches fresh; SDE may optionally hoist the drop above the reconcile loop). TESTS specified (mirror scan-api/scan-clobber harness, no FFmpeg): (1) HEADLINE DELETE-during-scan => id stays deleted, fails pre-fix/passes post-fix; (2) mount-loss-retained entry NOT dropped (false-positive guard); (3) genuinely-new scanned file still added; (4) non-concurrent case unchanged. HR1b SDE task remains PENDING per sequencing (runs AFTER HR1a build-verifies). D note is READY — coordinator informed.

HR1a (mechanical cluster) DONE + build-verified (coordinator-relayed): build-specialist verdict VERIFIED READY. lint 0; npm test 212/212; clean exit twice (process-level unhandledRejection/uncaughtException handlers don't keep the runner alive). All fixes confirmed REAL guards: A (4 routes try/catch -> 500 with disk-failure-injection tests; the process.on handlers log-only), B (saveDatabase rethrows -> updateDatabase rejects on disk failure, original intact, no orphan), C (recordServed sets persistedServedAt only when the entry exists — no-leak test fails against the old ordering, hot-path throttle intact), loadDatabase backfills ALL top-level keys (partial-db test passes), E (cleanupOrphanDbTmp startup sweep), F (durability comment softened). CONFIRMED UNTOUCHED: updateDatabase primitive body, scan Phase-2 membership reconcile / mergeScannedMetadata, transcode-cache.test.js (empty diff). All v1.8.0 + hardening headline tests still green. Marked HR1a status=done with result recorded. Advanced HR1b (now unblocked: HR1a build-verified AND the finding-D PE note already landed — the required sequencing since both edit the runScanDirectories tail): set HR1b status=in-progress and recorded the settled PE rule on the task (pe_rule). Re-verified the exact seams for the inbox: db.metadata is the scan-start Phase-1 snapshot (loadDatabase ~825), newMetadata initialized at server.js:941-942 (a clean spot to capture phase1Ids), the mergeScannedMetadata call is at server.js:1021 inside the Phase-2 mutator. Wrote software-developer inbox for HR1b ONLY, implementing the PE's finding-D reconciliation EXACTLY: capture `const phase1Ids = new Set(Object.keys(db.metadata))` at Phase-1 closed into the mutator; a drop loop over Object.keys(newMetadata) deleting newMetadata[id] when `phase1Ids.has(id) && !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(fresh.metadata, id)`, placed immediately BEFORE the mergeScannedMetadata call (may be hoisted above the reconcile loop per the PE's optional note). DO-NOT-TOUCH reiterated: mergeScannedMetadata body byte-unchanged; updateDatabase body unchanged; don't disturb HR1a's error handling/backfill/sweep. 4 integration regression tests (mirror scan-clobber/scan-api, no FFmpeg): (1) HEADLINE — a DELETE committing during a scan (after Phase-1 builds newMetadata, before Phase-2 commits) leaves the id DELETED, verified to FAIL pre-fix; (2) a mount-loss-retained entry NOT wrongly dropped (false-positive guard); (3) a genuinely-new scanned file still added; (4) the non-concurrent case unchanged. Constraints: transcode-cache.test.js frozen; full suite green (212 + new); every timer unref'd; PATH export before npm. Awaiting HR1b completion, then /prep-build-verify; after HR1b build-verifies -> coordinator runs a FOCUSED re-review of the combined HR1 hunks -> if clean, T3 (logo) -> PR.

HR1b (finding D) DONE + build-verified + committed in HR1 commit 7520d60 — marked HR1b status=done. The FOCUSED re-review of the HR1 commit then ran: verdict CONVERGING — 3 findings dedup to 2 distinct issues, DESCENDING severity. Coordinator DECISION (agreed): fix ONE (HR2), tech-debt-track the other (#4), then STOP the review cycle — this branch has had the full two-reviewer gate + 2 re-review passes and each layer found strictly smaller things (diminishing returns; the monotonic-convergence stop line, same discipline as v1.8.0's round-3 stop). Set stage remains implementation (fix round HR2). HR2 (FIX — hot-path regression that finding-C's HR1a fix introduced, CONFIRMED server.js 582-601): finding-C moved persistedServedAt.set(id,now) from BEFORE the updateDatabase enqueue to INSIDE the mutator (594/598). While dbWriteChain is backlogged (e.g. during a scan), persistedServedAt.get(id) stays undefined -> a burst of same-id /video Range requests each pass the hot-path short-circuit (585) and enqueue their own updateDatabase -> each does a full synchronous loadDatabase() in the lock, reintroducing the redundant-read throttle regression the v1.8.0 persistedServedAt map was built to prevent. Bounded (drains turn-by-turn; only the first commit writes) but on the streaming hot path, reachable whenever a scan/backlog overlaps streaming. FIX satisfies BOTH throttle + finding-C no-leak: restore the up-front optimistic persistedServedAt.set(id,now) BEFORE the enqueue (burst de-dup) AND persistedServedAt.delete(id) in the mutator's `if (!entry) return false` no-entry branch (undo the optimistic set so a concurrently-deleted id leaks nothing). Recorded HR2 in state.tasks, status=in-progress. TECH-DEBT (tracked, NOT fixed): added tech-debt-tracker.md Active #4 — the HR1b same-path re-add drop (server.js ~1086): a DELETE + a NEW file at the SAME path during a scan (same md5 id) is dropped for ONE scan cycle (fresh lacks the id, phase1Ids has it) -> re-added file invisible until the next scan (<=30m auto-scan On); transient/self-healing, NO permanent loss, inherent resurrect-vs-drop ambiguity of same-path/same-id; proposed future fix = before dropping, check the file currently exists on disk / was freshly re-scanned this pass and keep it if so. Wrote software-developer inbox for HR2 ONLY, grounded in the CURRENT recordServed (582-601): the exact two-part change (optimistic set after 585 short-circuit; delete in the 588 no-entry branch), the DO-NOT-TOUCH list (updateDatabase/mergeScannedMetadata bodies, HR1a route try/catch, HR1b drop loop), and the 2 tests ((a) finding-C no-leak still passes with the undo; (b) NEW burst-while-queued enqueues only ONE updateDatabase, fails against the current in-mutator-only ordering). Constraints: transcode-cache.test.js frozen; suite green (216 + new); PATH export before npm; don't touch T3. Awaiting HR2 completion, then /prep-build-verify; after HR2 build-verifies the coordinator does a TARGETED re-review of JUST the recordServed function (not a full gate) — if clean, T3 (logo) -> PR (review cycle STOPS here).
